title: ALPR San Jose Data (San Jose Police Department)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. The raw number of ALPR cameras (all ALPR cameras, not just those under the brand name “FLOCK”) active in San Jose, CA as of January 1, 2026.
2. The names of agencies and organizations with which the City of San Jose and/or the San Jose Police Department shared ALPR data in 2025;
3. The names of agencies and organizations from which the City of San Jose and/or the San Jose Police Department received ALPR data in 2025;
4. The names of agencies and organizations with which the City of San Jose and/or the San Jose Police Department shared any “hot list” information in 2025;
5. The names of agencies and organizations from which the City of San Jose and/or the San Jose Police Department received any “hot list” information in 2025.
6. A definition of the term “hot list” in this context.
7. The aggregate number of "detections" collected during 2025. If the City of San Jose and/or the San Jose Police Department has a retention period shorter than 1 year, please provide whatever data is available based on your retention period. For example, most Flock Safety systems may not have this data beyond 30 days.
8. The aggregate number of “hits” during 2025. If the City of San Jose and/or the San Jose Police Department has a retention period shorter than 1 year, please provide whatever data is available based on your retention period. For example, most Flock Safety systems may not have this data beyond 30 days.
9. Average Daily Scans based on the past amount of days in which you retain data.
10. A description of the information technology and physical location in which the records exist.
12. Any information or documentation about the City of San Jose and/or the San Jose Police Department’s use or potential use of a product called Flock Nova, which combines information from agencies into one place, such as “Unify CAD, RMS, video, LPR, and public records.” It is mentioned in this article (https://www.404media.co/license-plate-reader-company-flock-is-building-a-massive-people-lookup-tool-leak-shows/).
13. Please send a document of the most up-to-date ALPR policy that the City of San Jose and/or the San Jose Police Department uses.
14. The location (address, intersection, coordinates, or nearest landmark) of each ALPR camera operated, maintained, or accessed by the City of San Jose and/or the San Jose Police Department as of the date of this request.
15. If the City of San Jose and/or the San Jose Police Department uses mobile ALPR cameras (e.g., on patrol cars), please indicate how many mobile units are currently deployed, and describe any policies that govern how locations of scans from mobile units are logged or stored.
16. If exact addresses or coordinates are withheld, please provide general placement descriptions (e.g., "fixed camera at Main St & 2nd Ave," or "mobile ALPR unit assigned to Northern District patrol vehicle").
17. Any internal memos, emails, reports, or presentations from 2022–2025 discussing where ALPR cameras should be placed or were ultimately placed. This includes communications from officers, command staff, city officials, or vendor representatives regarding:
- Placement location recommendations
- Crime statistics or justifications used to determine placement
- Community concerns or opposition
- Equity or privacy impacts
- “High crime” area designations or related targeting rationale

CPRA requires you to undertake reasonable efforts to locate responsive records and to work in good faith with requesters to respond to their request. See CYAC v. City of National City, 220 Cal.App.4th 1385, 1430 (2013). Thus under the CPRA you are obligated to conduct a reasonable search and cannot deny a request merely because it might generate a large volume of records.
We ask that you please respond to this request within 10 days either by providing all the requested records or by providing a written response setting forth the legal authority on which you rely in withholding or redacting any document, as well as stating when documents will be made available. Should you choose to withhold or reject this request, we ask that you provide an explanation of why the City of San Jose and/or the San Jose Police Department is responding differently compared to the many others that have complied with our request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Anonymous Anon

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Dakota Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I would like to learn: 1) which local, state and national law enforcement agencies can currently submit probe photos to be run through the Bureau of Criminal Investigation's face recognition system, and 2) which law enforcement agencies actually submitted probe photos to the Bureau for a search from Jan. 1, 2025 to the date of the most recently available data.

title: Anthropic DoD Dispute

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Anthropic DoD Dispute
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Any final version of the January 9, 2026, memorandum issued by Secretary Hegseth regarding artificial intelligence use by the Department of Defense.
2. All final legal opinions, briefing materials, or decision memos regarding the use of the Defense Production Act (DPA) or the designation of Anthropic following the breakdown of negotiations on February 24, 2026.
3. All minutes, summaries, or preparatory talking pointsfor the February 24, 2026, meeting between Secretary Pete Hegseth and Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei.
4. All Joint Staff directives, EXORDs (Execute Orders), or planning memoranda from December 2025 to January 2026 regarding the deployment of the Claude AI model (via Palantir) for the mission to capture Nicolás Maduro.
5. Any correspondence within the Joint Staff J-2 (Intelligence) or J-3 (Operations) regarding safety guardrails or usage restrictions in the Claude model that were identified as blocking lethal operations or surveillance missions during early 2026.
6. All records of communication (including but not limited to emails, Signal/encrypted messages, SMS/text messages, and written memos) between Secretary Pete Hegseth and the following individuals from January 1, 2026, to the present, containing the terms "Anthropic," "Amodei," "guardrails," "Maduro," "artificial intelligence," "AI," or "unrestricted": (a) Cameron Stanley (Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Officer) and (b) Andrew Maples (Acting Principal Deputy Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Officer)

I request expedited processing pursuant to 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(6)(E) and 32 C.F.R. § 286.8(e) for the foregoing reasons:
1. Urgency to Inform: There is an urgent need to inform the public about actual Federal Government activity involving a 5:01 PM deadline today (Feb 27, 2026) that threatens to terminate major defense AI partnerships.
2. Media Interest: This matter is a subject of widespread and exceptional media interest (e.g., Axios, NYT reports today) involving potential questions about the government’s integrity and its adherence to ethical AI standards.
3. Imminent Threat: The dispute concerns the removal of safety guardrails related to lethal autonomous weapons and mass surveillance, a matter of grave public safety and constitutional concern.
I certify that the information contained in this request for expedited processing is true and correct to the best of my knowledge and belief.

I request a waiver of all search and duplication fees for the foregoing reasons:

1. Subject of Interest: The records concern the operations of the DoD and the use of the Defense Production Act.

2. Informative Value: Disclosure will contribute significantly to public understanding of how the DoD manages AI safety and "supply chain risk."

3. Public Understanding: I will disseminate these records via MuckRock, which is a public-facing platform used by journalists and researchers to inform the broad public.

4. Non-Commercial: I have no commercial interest in these records; my intent is purely to facilitate public transparency.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Carlos Gamez

title: umass

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: umass
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

All Advisories, Suspicious Activity Reports, Tips, Intelligence Briefs, Intel Reports, Leads Information or Data, Maps, Bulletins, BOLOs, and/or Joint Bulletins from November 25 2025 to the date this request is processed (inclusive) regarding National Security Presidential Memo 7 (NSPM-7). Please use the following query terms, "NSPM7" and "nspm-7" in addition to the following threat vectors referenced in the memorandum: "anti-Americanism", "anti-american", "traditional American" "nihilistic" "economic grievance" as search terms.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Daniel Boguslaw

title: Danny Casolaro

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Danny Casolaro
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any and all files on "Joseph Daniel 'Danny' Casolaro" (June 16, 1947 – August 10, 1991). West Virginia State Police investigated Danny Casolaro's death at sthe Sheraton Hotel in Martinsburg, West Virginia, on August 10, 1991. For your search purposes, alternative spellings of Danny Casolaro's name may include:

+ "J. Casolaro"
+ "J. Daniel Casolaro"
+ "Daniel Casolaro"

title: FBI Memos Trump Epstein (Department of Justice, Office of the Attorney General)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of the FBI memos referenced in this New York Times story: https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/25/us/politics/trump-epstein-files.html Specifically, I seek a copy of any and all "F.B.I. memos summarizing interviews the bureau did in connection to claims made in 2019 by a woman who came forward after Mr. Epstein’s arrest to say she had been sexually assaulted by both Mr. Trump and Mr. Epstein decades earlier, when she was a minor." The FBI wrote four such memos, according to the New York Times report, but three of them have not been released publicly. We seek to obtain those three memos. For more information about this case, see the attached document. 

I am a reporter for The Nation magazine and other news outlets, and seek a fee waiver. If that waiver is denied, I am willing to pay $25 for these records.

I seek expedited processing for this request. The information I seek could shed light on a matter of urgent and compelling public interest -- namely, the potential involvement of federal government personnel in the alleged sex trafficking operation organized by the late Jeffrey Epstein. This information is at the center of public discourse at this moment, and the Epstein case is a matter that involves the White House, Congress and numerous judicial bodies. It is imperative that the public obtain information on this matter in a timely matter, particularly given the corrosive effects the Epstein case has had on trust in government. For the reasons above, I ask that my request be given expedited processing because there is an urgency to inform the public about actual or alleged federal government activity, and I am a person primarily engaged in disseminating information. I intend to use the requested records to inform the public about actual government activity. I certify the foregoing to be true and correct to the best of my knowledge and belief.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jimmy Tobias

title: Use of Drones and Facial Recognition Technology

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

1. any requests for proposals, proposals submitted by vendors, contracts, budgets or cost allocations for the purchase, research of and/or use of aerial drones, UAs, UAVs, and UASs (hereinafter “drones”);

2. any policies, guidelines, manuals and/or instructions on use of drones, including on the legal process required (such as a warrant or court order), if any, before operating a drone;

3. any departmental records concerning use of drones now or plans to use drones in the future including:

a. the types of investigations or instances in which this institution is using or plans to use drones, or how it plans to support, manage or oversee the usage of drones by another department or office;

b. policies, guidelines, manuals and/or instructions on storage requirements or procedures for video or static images obtained through use of drones, including retention times;

c. the altitude at which drones can or do fly;

d. drones’ ability to carry weapons.

4. Records and materials related to the solicitation, acquisition, and use of face recognition technology and related software and services. The software or services may be provided by AWS (Rekognition), DataWorks, NEC, and Rank One Computing; this request is applicable to these and any other company providing facial recognition services under consideration or contract with this agency.

a. Responsive materials include but are not limited to:
• Agreements: contracts, licensing agreements, nondisclosure agreements.
• Company relations and communications: records related to meetings or follow-up actions with any vendors, companies, or other private entities marketing face recognition to this agency for immigration, intelligence, law enforcement, or other use.
• Financial records: purchase orders, invoices, and other memoranda and documentation.
• Marketing records: All marketing materials - unsolicited, requested, or otherwise - acquired from vendors of face recognition technology.
• Policy records: any policy directives, guidance documents, memoranda, training materials, or similar records governing the use of face recognition technology for immigration, law enforcement, or other purposes. Any memoranda of understanding between this agency and other agencies to share data, access remote systems or other forms of information sharing with external agencies.
• Validation and accuracy: Records, reports, audits, and other documents sufficient to describe validation, accuracy, reliability, and policy compliance of the system.

5. Records relating to the purchase of a Flock Drone or Drones (UAS). Specifically:
1) Purchase orders, invoices, quotes, and proposals relating to drones or unmanned aerial systems. (Just 1 document that shows costs and model #s)
2) Correspondence (emails, letters, memoranda) between JPD personnel and Flock personnel relating to the purchase of the UAS. Flock's email domain is "flocksafety.com".
3) JPD Internal operating procedures, presentations, and policy relating to the use and deployment of Flock UAS

Please limit the search to records produced from January 1, 2024 – present and to records held by the JPD. Please include in your search as responsive records: communications, memorandums, background papers, meeting minutes, email exchanges, or presentation materials.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Concerned Citizen

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

This is a request under the Freedom of Information Act. I am requesting access to and copies of the following historical intelligence reports regarding German underground installations, factories, and assembly plants during World War II.

These reports were generated by the Combined Intelligence Objectives Sub-committee (CIOS), the British Intelligence Objectives Sub-committee (BIOS), and the Joint Intelligence Objectives Agency (JIOA).
Requested Documents:

1. CIOS File Numbers:

    File No. II-12: Visit to Underground V-1 Manufacturing Plant (Sept 1944)

    File No. X-1: Caves at Bad Maries, used for Storage of Peroxide (Col. W.H. Wheeler)

    File No. XXIV-26, 27: Electronic Valve (Tube) Factories TeKaDe, Helmbrechts and Philips; Minden

    File No. XXVI-44: Messerschmidt Bombproof Assembly Plant Landsberg

    File No. XXVI-87: Dachs-I (Underground Lubricating Oil Plant) Porta, Westphalia

    File No. XXVII-59: Underground German Liquid Oxygen Plant, Wittring

    File No. XXVIII-45: Investigation of Group 2 Targets in Nordhausen Area

    File No. XXXII-15: Report on Visit to Kuckuck I Plant at Niedersachswerfen

    File No. XXXII-17: Underground Factories in Central Germany (Col. W.R.J. Cook)

2. CIOS Evaluation Reports:

    Evaluation Report No. 87: Investigation of Dannenberg V-1 Assembly Plant

    Evaluation Report No. 106: Target No. 28/5.55 Industriekontor, G.m.b.H.

    Evaluation Report No. 182: Underground Factory Sites in Ansbach

    Evaluation Report No. 238: Hauptausschuss Elektrotechnik (List of Underground Plants)

    Evaluation Report No. 252: Phillips Underground Valve Plant, Porta, Westfalia

3. JIOA Final Reports:

    Final Report No. 1: German Underground Installations. Part One: Unique Design and Construction

    Final Report No. 2: German Underground Installations. Part Two: Adaptations of Existing Facilities

    Final Report No. 3: German Underground Installations. Part Three: Various Installations

4. BIOS Final Reports:

    Final Report No. 65: Philips Valvo Works, Hamburg; Hammerwerke, Minden; C.H. Müller A.G., Hamburg

    Final Report No. 324: Band Oil Refinery at Porta (Nr. Minden)

communications.subject: Public Records Request :: P067024-022526
communications.communication:
Dear Michael Michael: 
Our office received your open records request pursuant to Missouri's Sunshine Law, Chapter 610, RSMo. Your request has been assigned number P067024-022526. The Patrol is committed to adhering to the Sunshine Law and to ensuring that the public obtains access to open records that are not otherwise closed by law. The Sunshine Law requires each public agency to adhere to certain standards. This letter is designed to ensure that you understand our process for fulfilling your records request. Three Business Day Notice Requirement: 
The requested records are not immediately available for dissemination or inspection. The Patrol is required to notify you that additional research time will be required to fulfill the request. This correspondence satisfies the Sunshine Law's notice requirement within the required three business day period. Section 610.023.3, RSMo. 
 Additional Research Time is Required: 
The Patrol receives thousands of open records requests each month. Our Patrol Records Division personnel are dedicated to meeting the intent of Missouri's Sunshine Law and strive to respond to each request in a timely manner. The Patrol operates in all 114 Missouri counties and the independent city of St. Louis. Accordingly, many records maintained by the agency are not centrally located within, or immediately accessible to, the Patrol's Custodian of Records in Jefferson City. Our staff must research your request and ensure that we have captured all responsive records  ̶  regardless of their location within the agency. The research process is time-consuming, but we will make every effort to expedite your request. Estimated Time for Completion: 
The majority of our requests are fulfilled within thirty business days. Some requests require additional research time due to their scope or complexity. Our team will conduct an initial review of your request. If we estimate that the request will be fulfilled within thirty business days, we will not send an updated time estimate. However, if we estimate that the request will take longer than thirty business days, we will provide an updated time estimate that provides a detailed justification for the additional research time. Billing: 
As it relates to billing, Missouri law allows the Patrol to charge fees for copies and research time. Specifically, "[r]esearch time required for fulfilling records requests may be charged at the actual cost of research time." Section 610.026, RSMo. The Missouri Supreme Court recently determined that "staff time is the time required to provide access to public records maintained on computer facilities, recording tapes or disks, videotapes or films, pictures, maps, slides, graphics, illustrations or similar audio or visual items". Gross v. Parson, 624 S.W.3d 877, 884-89 (Mo. 2021) (internal quotations omitted). 
The Patrol will never bill you for redaction time or any research time conducted by our legal staff. You will only be charged for copies and staff research time as permitted by Missouri law. Please let us know if you have questions related to the billing estimate. 
Our team will forward a bill estimate to your attention once we have calculated the permissible costs and fees. The Patrol will not provide the responsive records until receipt of payment. Section 610.026.2, RSMo. 
We appreciate your patience as we review, research, and fulfill your open records request. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you will receive an email when your request has been completed. If you wish to withdraw or clarify your request, or if you have questions, please call our office at (573) 526-6146, or (573) 526-6167. 
 
Sincerely, 
Gerard G. Callahan 
Custodian of Records 
 
Login Link: https://mshp.govqa.us/WEBAPP/_rs/Login.aspx

title: MA enterprise agreement with OpenAI

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: MA enterprise agreement with OpenAI
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Any documents related to a government contract with OpenAI referencing the enterprise-wide deployment of a ChatGPT-powered AI assistant. Documents could include the contract and/or enterprise agreement between Massachusetts and OpenAI.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Aarushi Sahejpal

title: PRA: Autopsy and coroner’s reports

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: PRA: Autopsy and coroner’s reports
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act (Government Code Section 6250 et seq.) ("CPRA”), and all other applicable laws, please disclose the following records.

Any and all autopsy, death investigation, and coroner’s reports relating to the deaths of the following individuals:

- Name: Garrett Schmidt, Date of birth: 05-27-1983, Date of death: 10-27-2016
-  Name: Victor Johnson, Date of birth: 11-03-1963, Date of death: 08-02-2018
- Name: Stephen Murray, Date of birth: 01-12-1967, Date of death: 07-17-2019

If records pursuant to this request are possessed in machine-readable format such as an .xls, .csv spreadsheet file, please provide the record in this format. Please disclose these records in the format in which they are kept, e.g., .ppt, .pptx, .doc, or .docx.

The fundamental rule of the CPRA is a presumption of public access. “In other words, [A]ll public records are subject to disclosure unless the Legislature has expressly provided to the contrary.” Williams v. Superior Court, 5 Cal. 4th 337 (1993). This presumption finds further support in the California Constitution, as amended by Proposition 59 in 2004. “A statute, court rule, or other authority, including those in effect on the effective date of this subdivision, shall be broadly construed if it furthers the people's right of access, and narrowly construed if it limits the right of access.” Cal. Const. Art. 1 § 3(b)(2).

Please provide a determination on this request within 10 days of your receipt of it. If you determine that any or all of the information qualifies for an exemption from disclosure, please note whether, as is normally the case, the exemption is discretionary, and if so whether it is necessary in this case to exercise your discretion to withhold the information. If you determine that some but not all of the information is exempt from disclosure and that you intend to withhold it, please redact it for the time being and make the rest available as requested. In any event, please provide a signed notification citing the legal authorities on which you rely if you determine that any or all of the information is exempt and will not be disclosed.

Please apply a fee waiver to this request. If the request for a fee waiver is denied, please provide notification of any duplication costs exceeding $50 before you duplicate the records. If the request for a fee waiver is denied, please further provide an index of all other requests since 2018 for which a fee waiver was denied, and the reason therefore.

Please respond to this request and disclose the records electronically by email to this MuckRock email address. Please let us know if you have any questions or if we can help ensure this request is the least burdensome possible for your agency while providing a correct and full disclosure in the public interest.

If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to reach out to me via email at dillon@muckrock.com, or by phone at (267) 314-7952.

Thank you.

Best,
Dillon Bergin
